source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A merry-go-round (MGR) has two orthogonal axes ($x, y$) painted on it, and is rotating on the earth (assume to be an inertial frame $x_{0}, y_{0}, z_{0}$) with constant angular velocity $\omega$ about a vertical axis. A bug of mass $m$ is crawling outward without slipping along the $x$-axis with constant velocity $v_{0}$ (Fig. 1.75). What is the total force $\mathbf{F}_{b}$ exerted by the MGR on the bug? Give all components of $\mathbf{F}_{b}$ in the earth-frame coordinates $x_{0}, y_{0}, z_{0}$ of the bug.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $\mathbf{F}_{b}=-m v_{0} \omega\left[2\sin(\omega t)+\omega t \cos(\omega t)\right]\mathbf{i}+m v_{0} \omega\left[2\cos(\omega t)-\omega t \sin(\omega t)\right]\mathbf{j}+m g\mathbf{k}$
extraction_notes: question references "Fig. 1.75" but record marks vision_relevance as optional

multi_step: Solving requires combining rotating-frame/earth-frame kinematics with force determination, not a single direct lookup
The asked answer includes all force components in `x0, y0, z0`, which requires transforming motion into earth-frame coordinates
The setup combines multiple conditions: constant angular velocity, outward crawling along the rotating `x`-axis, no slipping, and gravity
verifiability: The answer is a closed-form vector expression with explicit `i`, `j`, and `k` components, which is straightforward to compare against a derived result
`answer_present=true`, `answer_type=short_text`, and the provided answer has concrete mathematical content rather than an open-ended explanation
`registry_passed=true` supports that the core text/answer assets are available for checking

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A merry-go-round (MGR) has two orthogonal axes ($x, y$) painted on it, and is rotating on the earth (assume to be an inertial frame $x_{0}, y_{0}, z_{0}$) with constant angular velocity $\omega$ about a vertical axis. A bug of mass $m$ is crawling outward without slipping along the $x$-axis with constant velocity $v_{0}$ (Fig. 1.75). What is the total force $\mathbf{F}_{b}$ exerted by the MGR on the bug? Give all components of $\mathbf{F}_{b}$ in the earth-frame coordinates $x_{0}, y_{0}, z_{0}$ of the bug.
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text: $\mathbf{F}_{b}=-m v_{0} \omega\left[2\sin(\omega t)+\omega t \cos(\omega t)\right]\mathbf{i}+m v_{0} \omega\left[2\cos(\omega t)-\omega t \sin(\omega t)\right]\mathbf{j}+m g\mathbf{k}$
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: question references "Fig. 1.75" but record marks vision_relevance as optional
image field contains a PIL image representation string rather than a concrete file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: In the rotating coordinate system $(x, y, z)$, the bug, which crawls with constant velocity $v_{0}$ along the $x$-axis, has no acceleration, so that the horizontal force acting on it by the MGR is (Problem 1094) $\mathbf{F}=2 m \omega \times \mathbf{v}^{\prime}+m \omega \times(\boldsymbol{\omega} \times \mathbf{r})$, where $\omega=\omega \mathbf{e}_{z}$, $\mathbf{v}^{\prime}=v_{0} \mathbf{e}_{x}$, $\mathbf{r}=x \mathbf{e}_{x}$. The bug has a weight $-m g \mathbf{e}_{z}$, so that the MGR exerts a reaction force $m g \mathbf{e}_{z}$ on the bug. Hence the total force exerted by the MGR on the bug is $\mathbf{F}_{b}=2 m v_{0} \omega \mathbf{e}_{y}-m \omega^{2} x \mathbf{e}_{x}+m g \mathbf{e}_{z}$. Choose the earth frame $(x_{0}, y_{0}, z_{0})$ such that at $t=0$, the corresponding axes coincide with those of the rotating frame. Then, denoting the unit vectors along the $x_{0^{-}}, y_{0^{-}}, z_{0^{-}}$ axes by $\mathbf{i}, \mathbf{j}, \mathbf{k}$ respectively, we have $\mathbf{e}_{x}=\cos(\omega t) \mathbf{i}+\sin(\omega t) \mathbf{j}$, $\mathbf{e}_{y}=-\sin(\omega t) \mathbf{i}+\cos(\omega t) \mathbf{j}$, $\mathbf{e}_{z}=\mathbf{k}$. For simplicity, assume that the bug was at the origin at $t=0$, then $x=v_{0} t$. In the earth frame, $\mathbf{F}_{b}$ can thus be written as $\mathbf{F}_{b}=-m v_{0} \omega\left[2\sin(\omega t)+\omega t \cos(\omega t)\right]\mathbf{i}+m v_{0} \omega\left[2\cos(\omega t)-\omega t \sin(\omega t)\right]\mathbf{j}+m g \mathbf{k}$.

open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text: A merry-go-round (MGR) has two orthogonal axes ($x, y$) painted on it, and is rotating on the earth (assume to be an inertial frame $x_{0}, y_{0}, z_{0}$) with constant angular velocity $\omega$ about a vertical axis. A bug of mass $m$ is crawling outward without slipping along the $x$-axis with constant velocity $v_{0}$. What is the total force $\mathbf{F}_{b}$ exerted by the MGR on the bug? Give all components of $\mathbf{F}_{b}$ in the earth-frame coordinates $x_{0}, y_{0}, z_{0}$ of the bug.
open_ended_problem_variants.expected_answer: $\mathbf{F}_{b}=-m v_{0} \omega\left[2\sin(\omega t)+\omega t \cos(\omega t)\right]\mathbf{i}+m v_{0} \omega\left[2\cos(\omega t)-\omega t \sin(\omega t)\right]\mathbf{j}+m g\mathbf{k}$
